summary: DHH notes that Apple has begun selling the iLife bundle but still hasn't made it available for download, despite earlier promises. The global Apple site moved from pre-ordering to selling but remains vague about download availability. Meanwhile, the Danish Apple site is about to miss its own 'late January' download deadline. DHH concludes Apple could have handled the rollout communication better.
key_points: Apple transitioned iLife from pre-order to sale without providing promised downloads
The global site used vague language about download availability while the Danish site made a specific deadline it was about to miss
Apple's inconsistent messaging across regional sites created a poor customer experience
money_quotes: Tracking progress on the iLife bundle is an excercise is subtlety.
Apple could have handled this better.
key_insight: Apple's vague and inconsistent communication across regional sites around the iLife launch showed a lack of attention to customer expectations.
